arguments.task: Research Telegram using official first-party sources. Open core.telegram.org and find pages that document Telegram Bot API, MTProto/API access, authentication requirements, rate limits, and any pricing or access/gating information. Also find official links for creating bots/tokens and any GitHub or MCP-related official pages if available. Return concise findings with exact source URLs and quoted supporting text snippets where possible. Do not use third-party blogs unless necessary for MCP registry evidence.

input.thought: Start a browser research run on Telegram official docs and related first-party sources.

raw_result.description: Messaging platform with public Bot API and Telegram API/MTProto interfaces for bots and client apps.
raw_result.auth_notes: Bot API uses a bot token obtained from @BotFather; Telegram API/MTProto requires registering an application to obtain api_id and api_hash tied to a developer account. The exact auth flow for end-user login/session creation in MTProto was not fully verified in the gathered evidence.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official Telegram developer docs describe self-serve bot creation via @BotFather and self-serve application registration for api_id/api_hash; no contact-sales or partner approval gate was found in the gathered evidence.

api_surface.breadth: Telegram exposes a broad HTTP Bot API for messaging, updates, files, webhooks, commands, and bot management, plus a separate Telegram API based on MTProto for full client functionality. Because the assignment requires one API surface type enum and the gathered evidence centered on HTTP Bot API plus non-GraphQL MTProto docs, the classification here is REST with broader non-REST capabilities noted.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Telegram MCP server or official MCP registry entry was verified in the gathered evidence. Official GitHub evidence was found for TDLib/Telegram libraries, not MCP.

raw_result.blocker: A first bot toolkit is buildable from the public Bot API today, but a full Telegram toolkit is not purely straightforward because Telegram has two surfaces (Bot API and MTProto), and the broader MTProto side uses a different auth/protocol model that was only partially verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official error documentation includes flood-wait style limits such as FLOOD_WAIT_X and FLOOD_PREMIUM_WAIT_X for Telegram API/MTProto. A precise Bot API numeric rate-limit policy was not verified from the gathered evidence, so implementers should expect anti-abuse throttling and handle retry/backoff.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs present multiple developer surfaces on the main platform page (Bot API, Telegram API/TDLib, Gateway API). Telegram maintains an official TDLib GitHub repository, which is a positive signal for ecosystem maturity. Self-serve bot token issuance via @BotFather is a strong signal that bot integrations are practical today.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some fields remain ambiguous from the gathered evidence alone: the strict single enum for API surface type does not perfectly capture both HTTP Bot API and MTProto; no official MCP registry/source was verified; and precise Bot API numeric rate-limit details were not confirmed.
